title: Analysis of Islet Function by Glucagon Enzyme-linked Immunosorbent Assay (ELISA)
author: IIDP-HIPP
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bz9zp976

steps.title: Preparation of Samples, Standards, and internal Quality Controls
steps.instruction: Preparation of Samples, Standards, and internal Quality Controls

steps.title: Thaw archived samples intended for analysis in room temperature water
steps.instruction: Thaw archived samples intended for analysis in room temperature water. Once thawed, invert capped samples ten times to thoroughly mix.

steps.title: Bring the Glucagon Standard to room temperature
steps.instruction: Bring the Glucagon Standard to room temperature. Keep remaining kit components between 2-8°C.

steps.title: Retrieve the islet hormone extracts and keep on ice
steps.instruction: Retrieve the islet hormone extracts and keep on ice.

steps.title: Prepare serial dilutions of hormone extract...
steps.instruction: Prepare serial dilutions of hormone extract (1:100, 1:1000, 1:2000, 1:5000 and 1:10000) in 2mL tubes using the Calibrator Diluent RD5-59 from the ELISA Kit. Vortex each tube to mix contents thoroughly before pipetting the subsequent dilutions.

steps.title: Prepare the glucagon standard dilutions using Calibrator Diluent RD5-59
steps.instruction: Prepare the glucagon standard dilutions using Calibrator Diluent RD5-59. Perform a 1:10 dilution starting from the 20,000 pg/mL standard vial followed by six 1:2 dilutions. Vortex each tube to mix contents thoroughly before pipetting the subsequent dilutions.

steps.title: Reconstitute each Quality Control stock in...
steps.instruction: Reconstitute each Quality Control stock in 5 mL distilled water, or thaw existing quality control samples.

steps.title: Prepare the wash buffer dilution by...
steps.instruction: Prepare the wash buffer dilution by adding 100 mL of buffer concentrate to 900 mL of DI water.

steps.title: Prior to beginning the assay, use...
steps.instruction: Prior to beginning the assay, use the plate washer to soak plate wells in 800 µL diluted wash buffer for 30 seconds. Wash and aspirate the plate twice with 400 µL wash buffer.

steps.title: Performing Glucagon ELISA assay
steps.instruction: Performing Glucagon ELISA assay

steps.title: Add 150 µL of Assay Diluent RD1-110 to each well
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL of Assay Diluent RD1-110 to each well.

steps.title: By using the epMotion 5057 or...
steps.instruction: By using the epMotion 5057 or hand-pipetting, add 50 µL of standards and controls (in duplicate), samples, and extract dilutions to the wells.

steps.title: Cover plate with the supplied adhesive...
steps.instruction: Cover plate with the supplied adhesive strip and incubate for 3 hours at room temperature.

steps.title: Using the plate washer, aspirate each...
steps.instruction: Using the plate washer, aspirate each well and wash as directed in step1.8. Repeat 3 times.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of ice-cold Glucagon Conjugate to each well
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of ice-cold Glucagon Conjugate to each well.

steps.title: Cover plate with a new adhesive...
steps.instruction: Cover plate with a new adhesive strip and incubate for 1 hour at 4 °C . Do not stack the plates if running more than one kit.

steps.title: Using the plate washer, repeat the wash/aspiration step 2
steps.instruction: Using the plate washer, repeat the wash/aspiration step 2.4.

steps.title: Within 15 minutes of use, combine...
steps.instruction: Within 15 minutes of use, combine Color Reagents A and B in equal volume, mix solution thoroughly, and add 200 µL to each well.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature away from light
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature away from light.

steps.title: Add 50 µL Stop Solution to each well
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL Stop Solution to each well.

steps.title: Using the microplate reader, determine the...
steps.instruction: Using the microplate reader, determine the optical density and glucagon concentration of each well within 30 minutes of adding stop solution. Set to 450 nm with a wavelength correction at 540 nm.

steps.title: Data Analysis
steps.instruction: Data Analysis

steps.title: Values for all standards must be...
steps.instruction: Values for all standards must be within ±15% of their expected values and replicate values of each standard must have a Coefficient of Variation (CV) ≤20%. If standards vary beyond these limits, the assay must be repeated.

steps.title: Values for quality control samples, corresponding...
steps.instruction: Values for quality control samples, corresponding to lower and upper assay detection ranges, must be within their known ranges. If QCs vary beyond these limits, the assay must be repeated.

steps.title: Calculate the average of the glucagon...
steps.instruction: Calculate the average of the glucagon concentrations from the 4 extract dilutions to determine glucagon content, expressed as pg/mL.

steps.title: Normalize secreted glucagon concentrations per islet...
steps.instruction: Normalize secreted glucagon concentrations per islet volume (IEQs), expressed as pg/100 IEQs/min and islet glucagon content, expressed as % content/min.

steps.title: Use Prism software to create graphs...
steps.instruction: Use Prism software to create graphs and to calculate stimulation index (SI) and area under curve (AUC) values. 3.5.1 Stimulation index (SI) is a ratio calculated as maximum response to a given stimulus relative to baseline. 3.5.2 Area under curve (AUC) is calculated by integrating islet secretory response to a given stimulus over time.

steps.title: Data Storage and Reporting
steps.instruction: Data Storage and Reporting

steps.title: To facilitate data management and ensure...
steps.instruction: To facilitate data management and ensure data security, the VUMC HIPP uses an institutional server-based platform for data storage and analysis.

steps.title: Upon analysis completion, the VUMC HIPP...
steps.instruction: Upon analysis completion, the VUMC HIPP will upload raw data, including hormone levels, data analysis, and graphical representation of each human islet perifusion, into the IIDP HIPP database. Example of human islet perifusion results performed in HIPP is shown in Figure 1 . Figure 1. Protocol for analysis of human islet function by the Vanderbilt HIPP. Islets are challenged with 16.7 mM glucose for 30 minutes to resolve biphasic insulin secretory response, followed by 15-minute stimulations with 16.7 mM glucose + 50 M IBMX, 1.7 mM glucose + 1 M epinephrine, and 20 mM KCl. Insulin secretory response to these stimuli is shown in the top panel (5 human islet preparations) and glucagon secretion is displayed in the bottom panel (4 human islet preparations).

steps.title: Functional data on islet insulin and...
steps.instruction: Functional data on islet insulin and glucagon secretion will be uploaded within 3 business days to HIPP database built by IIDP programming team and immediately be available to IIDP-affiliated investigators and islet isolation centers.

steps.title: Document any deviations that occurred during...
steps.instruction: Document any deviations that occurred during this protocol that affect the final results and report with the analysis of the assay.
